fuzzy_collisions.reason: tokens 'device' + 'id' match existing IoT-domain columns; potential semantic overlap with device_battery_map and battery_alerts
tokens 'serial' + 'number' identical to existing battery serial columns; intended as logical FK to inventory.serial_number per BRD 6.2.4
tokens 'dealer' + 'id' match standard dealer FK pattern across many tables
inventory.soc_percent already holds last-known SOC for a battery — overlap with iot_devices.soc_percent indicates duplicated state
tokens 'voltage' + 'v' match an existing column on products (nominal voltage); semantically distinct (instantaneous vs nominal) but reviewer should confirm

notes: BRD sync audit (line 487-495 of /tmp/nbfc_extract/sync_audit.txt) lists 'IoT telemetry ingestion' and 'iot_devices dealer_id fix in 6.S.5' as IN SYNC items — confirming iot_devices is referenced across BRDs but not yet defined in schema.ts.

Existing schema already has device_battery_map (device_id, battery_serial, dealer_id, status, installed_at) and inventory.soc_percent + inventory.soc_last_sync_at + inventory.iot_imei_no. Reviewer must decide whether iot_devices is additive (current-state cache) vs absorbing fields into inventory/device_battery_map.

Fuzzy collisions on 'serial_number' and 'dealer_id' are intentional FK-style reuse (BRD 6.2.4 documents them as logical FKs). They are flagged because the strict ≥0.6 Jaccard rule treats every same-named column as a collision; reviewer should rule on whether to accept this pattern as 'FK reuse' rather than rename.
Boilerplate columns (id, created_at, updated_at) were excluded from the fuzzy-collision count since they are universal Drizzle conventions.
